body_txt: Not all demons are Big Red Devils or massive draconic beasts who seek to terrify or destroy every human they meet. Some fiends like to use a more subtle approach to their machinations and take the form of a beautiful woman or a handsome man when going among mortals. Demons often want to tempt mortals into committing sin, and one of the ways they do it is through adopting a pleasing form, linking the metaphorical seduction by evil that a person faces to a literal seduction attempt. While sometimes it will be as blatant as a succubus or incubus trying to jump the hero's bones, in others it will simply be an additional way to throw the hero off their game by taking a form the demon knows they're attracted to, thus making it harder for them to bring themselves to hurt the demon. The origins for this trope have their roots in the idea that Beauty Equals Goodness , one that is deeply ingrained in culture and which a demon will not hesitate to use to its advantage. Meanwhile, sex and sexuality are (in Christianity) often linked with evil (and evil itself is often depicted as an erotic, tempting force). This combines to mean that while angels usually have chaste, Angelic Beauty and modest white robes, demons will either be slinking around in tight red dresses or Dressed Like a Dominatrix . This idea is not new. There was notably a statue, L'ange du mal ( the angel of evil ), that portrayed Satan in a way that was perceived as way too handsome and was removed from the church and replaced by Le génie du mal out of fear that it would "pervert the thoughts of youngsters." Later, stories from witch trials often had witches claim that they mated with the Devil in the guise of a handsome man. Often overlaps with Divinely Appearing Demons (if they don't have pointed horns and a tail instead), Face of an Angel, Mind of a Demon (unless they're a Noble Demon ). If female, they're often a Cute Monster Girl (of which this is a Sub-Trope ) with their only outwardly demonic features being bat wings, horns, and a tail. Also a Sub-Trope of Our Demons Are Different .
